opendb_id.description: Unique identifier for the document (UUID v4)

socket.description: The CPU socket type supported by the motherboard. E.g. AM4, AM5, LGA 1700, LGA 2066, TR5, etc.

form_factor.description: The physical size and layout of the motherboard (e.g., ATX, Micro ATX, Mini-ITX)

chipset.description: The chipset used by the motherboard (e.g., Intel Z790, AMD X670E)

max.description: The maximum amount of RAM the motherboard can support in GB

ram_type.description: The type of RAM supported (e.g., DDR5, DDR4)

slots.description: The number of RAM slots on the motherboard

color.description: The color(s) of this product. Each color of the product should be included in the array. Includes PCB and heatsink colors

pcie_slots.description: PCIe slots on the motherboard. E.g. a motherboard with 4 PCIe x16 slots, 2 PCIe x8 slots, and 1 PCIe x4 slot would be represented as an array with 3 objects. Be careful on this section and use all available info to make your decision including obvious pcie slots on the motherboard images if provided.

quantity.description: Number of slots of this type and generation

lanes.description: Number of lanes per slot. E.g. 16 lanes for x16 slot, 8 lanes for x8 slot, etc.

m2_slots.description: M.2 slots on the motherboard. For every slot that is supported, add an object to the array. E.g. for a motherboard with 4 M.2 slots, add 4 objects to the array.

wireless_networking.description: Supported wifi standards. E.g. Wifi 6E, Wifi 7, etc

usb_headers.description: Internal USB headers. Please only include front panel headers. Do not include headers on the back of the motherboard.

usb_3_2_gen_1.description: Number of USB 3.2 Gen 1 headers. This is also known as USB 5Gbps.

usb_3_2_gen_2.description: Number of USB 3.2 Gen 2 headers. This is also known as USB 10Gbps.

usb_3_2_gen_2x2.description: Number of USB 3.2 Gen 2x2 headers. This is also known as USB 20Gbps.

usb_4.description: Number of USB 4 headers. This is also known as USB 40Gbps.

usb_4_80g.description: Number of USB 4 headers. This is also known as USB 80Gbps.

fan_headers.description: Fan and cooling headers on the motherboard

cpu_fan.description: Number of CPU fan headers (typically 4-pin PWM)

case_fan.description: Number of case/chassis fan headers (typically 4-pin PWM)

pump.description: Number of dedicated pump headers for AIO coolers

cpu_opt.description: Number of CPU optional fan headers

rgb_headers.description: RGB and lighting headers on the motherboard

power_button.description: Whether the motherboard has a power button header

reset_button.description: Whether the motherboard has a reset button header

power_led.description: Whether the motherboard has a power LED header

hdd_led.description: Whether the motherboard has an HDD activity LED header

cpu_power.description: CPU power connector specifications (e.g., ['8-pin', '8-pin'] for dual 8-pin)

ecc_support.description: Whether the motherboard supports ECC memory

raid_support.description: Whether the motherboard supports RAID configurations

back_connect_connectors.description: Whether the motherboard uses back-connect connectors

flashback.description: Whether the board has BIOS flashback capability

clear_cmos.description: Whether the board has a clear CMOS button

chipset.description: Details on the audio chipset used

back_panel_ports.description: This is a list of ports on the back of the motherboard.

general_product_information.description: General product information and retailer SKUs

series.description:
Product series name

The series is the manufacturer's sub-brand or product line that groups related boards. Manufacturer (e.g., ASUS, MSI, Gigabyte, ASRock), chipset (e.g., X870E, Z890, B650), and memory type (e.g., DDR5) are stored separately and used as the default variant grouping, so do NOT repeat any of them in the series. Examples: 'Asus ROG CROSSHAIR X870E HERO WIFI DDR5 ATX' → series='ROG CROSSHAIR'. 'Asus X870E-E ROG STRIX GAMING WIFI DDR5 ATX' → series='ROG STRIX'. 'Asus X870E ProArt CREATOR WIFI AM5 DDR5 ATX' → series='ProArt'. 'Asus X870E-PLUS TUF GAMING WIFI7 AM5 DDR5 ATX' → series='TUF Gaming'. 'MSI MEG X870E GODLIKE' → series='MEG'. 'Gigabyte X870E AORUS MASTER' → series='AORUS'. 'ASRock X870E Taichi' → series='Taichi'. The series is what stays consistent across multiple chipset generations of the same product line.

variant.description:
Specific variant in the series

The variant is the full board model identifier inside the series. It MUST include the chipset SKU as the manufacturer prints it (e.g., X870E, X870E-E, X870E-H, X870E-PLUS, B650M-A) plus any model descriptor that distinguishes it from siblings (HERO, APEX, EXTREME, NEO GAMING, GAMING, MASTER, ELITE, EDGE, CARBON, GLACIAL, BTF, ACE, Creator, etc.). Include WIFI / WIFI7 / BT tokens in the variant ONLY when they are part of the manufacturer's printed model name on the box (most modern ASUS/MSI/Gigabyte boards do print 'WIFI' or 'WIFI7' as part of the SKU). The full WiFi standard (Wi-Fi 6E, Wi-Fi 7, etc.) still goes into the separate `wireless_networking` field — `wireless_networking` describes the radio capability while the variant just preserves the SKU exactly as marketed. Do NOT include the manufacturer, the broad series, the form factor, or memory type — those are stored separately. Examples: 'Asus ROG CROSSHAIR X870E HERO WIFI DDR5 ATX' → variant='X870E HERO WIFI'. 'Asus X870E-E ROG STRIX GAMING WIFI DDR5 ATX' → variant='X870E-E NEO GAMING WIFI' (use the exact chipset suffix and model words shown on the box). 'Asus X870E ProArt CREATOR WIFI AM5 DDR5 ATX' → variant='X870E-Creator WIFI'. 'ASUS X870E ROG CROSSHAIR APEX' (no WIFI in name) → variant='X870E APEX'. 'Asus X870E ROG CROSSHAIR EXTREME' → variant='X870E EXTREME'. 'Asus X870E-PLUS TUF GAMING WIFI7' → variant='X870E-PLUS WIFI7'. 'Asus X870E-H ROG STRIX GAMING WIFI7' → variant='X870E-H Gaming WIFI7'. 'Asus X870E ROG CROSSHAIR HERO BTF WIFI' → variant='X870E HERO BTF WIFI'. 'ASUS ROG CROSSHAIR X870E GLACIAL' → variant='X870E GLACIAL'. The variant must be unique within a (manufacturer, chipset, memory type) group — if two boards would collapse to the same variant string, you are missing a model descriptor.

manufacturer_color.description:
Manufacturer's official color name for this product variant.

Use the manufacturer's marketed colorway name when one exists; otherwise derive a simple human-readable color from the product images of the motherboard PCB and heatsinks (do not infer color from words in the title). Most stock motherboards are 'Black'. Common simple values: 'Black', 'White', 'White / Silver', 'Silver'. Named special-edition colorways DO belong here when the manufacturer brands them as a distinct colorway: e.g. 'HATSUNE MIKU EDITION', 'HALO EDITION', 'NOCTUA EDITION', 'STAR WARS EDITION'. Examples: a standard ROG STRIX X870E-E in black → manufacturer_color='Black'. The white ROG CROSSHAIR X870E GLACIAL → manufacturer_color='White' (GLACIAL is the model variant, not the colorway). The Hatsune Miku ROG STRIX X870E-H Gaming → manufacturer_color='HATSUNE MIKU EDITION'. Do NOT put plain model descriptors like HERO, APEX, EXTREME, GLACIAL, BTF, NEO, GAMING, or Creator in manufacturer_color — those belong in the variant. Only put a name here if it is a manufacturer-branded colorway/finish, not a model tier.

last_manually_spec_verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the part specs were last manually verified by a human
